p{} element selecter
.__ {} class selector
#__ {} id selector
(element) , (element){} group selector 

In this file i use `` to refer an element 

`font family`
use google fonts to get fonts 

1px = 1/96 of inch 

there are three ways to write color on css 
1. color by name -->  red
2. color by rgb value --> rgb(0,0,0)  (btw black)
3. color by hex value --> #ffffff (btw white)

padding/maergin : 10px 20px 30px 40px  -->(top right bottom laft)
padding/maergin : 10px 20px -->(y,x)
box-sizing: border box;

imp prop ( display: inline-block )

there are main 5 main type of position 
position: absolute , relative , static(default) , sticky and fixed

(relative mean ralative to its normal position and will leave a gap at its normal position)
(absolute mean moving from the main position to its parent)
(sticky mean first to asign its position and then it will stick to the position like (navigation bar on web))
(sticky will frist stay at its asigned position and as we scroll down it will stick there)
